//: lint_duplicate_helpers baseline — module-level helper names defined in >=2 in-scope defender/ modules. Fingerprint is the bare name. CI fails on a dup name absent here. Regenerate: python scripts/lint/lint_duplicate_helpers.py --update-baseline. Shrink as dups are consolidated; annotate intentional entries, "" = un-triaged. Never hand-add to silence a new dup — fix it or use `# lint-dup: ok — <reason>`.

entries._author_to_author: distinct-ok: curator (observations) vs lessons/run.py (findings) batch result-partitioners — curator commits with Generation/model trailers + 2 buckets, lessons commits trailer-free + the extra held_forward_bad forward-check bucket (#386)
entries._config_path: distinct-ok: _stub_transport joins the shared systems/<system>/config.env path; elastic_adapter resolves its OWN config whose keys are ELASTICSEARCH_URL/KIBANA_URL, not the URL_BASE/BASTION_HOST/TIMEOUT_SEC template — different key set, so it cannot use the shared loader (#619, surfaced when the *_cli.py suffix exclusion was retired)
entries._json_safe: distinct-ok: serialize.py coerces YAML scalars (dates/sets) for JSON; sql.py maps non-finite floats to null for DuckDB — different problems
entries._partition_pre_author: distinct-ok: outcome-policy + held-out gate (curator, 3-tuple) vs ground-truth-disposition gate (lessons, 2-tuple) — different pre-author gating semantics (#386)
entries._policy: distinct-ok: bash_policy._policy loads the read-denylist JSON (no args); policy_cli._policy compiles an AgentPolicy for a given definition+run dir. Unrelated. Newly visible because policy_cli.py ends in _cli.py and was hidden by the retired suffix exclusion — pre-existing, not introduced by #619
entries._request: distinct-ok: stub_transport returns dict|list and sys.exit(2) (adapter exit-code protocol); ticket_writer returns (status, body) non-fatally — different contracts
entries._run: distinct-ok: generic subprocess-runner name; the harness pair share one, but sql.py's _run is a DuckDB executor and validate_scaffold's is its own — distinct bodies
entries._run_batch_inner: distinct-ok: curator vs lessons batch drivers — different partition shape, result buckets (held_forward_bad), and queue concurrency model; only the shared run_batch_envelope is unified (#386)
entries.capture: distinct-ok: harness_lead writes eval result artifacts; record_query runs an adapter into the queries table — unrelated roles, same name
entries.health_check: distinct-ok: three transports, three liveness questions — _stub_transport GETs /health over the shared HTTP path, elastic reports ES+Kibana as DATA (an unreachable Kibana must not trip the breaker), host_state lists containers over docker. Same verb name by contract; unrelated bodies (#619)
entries.invoke_agent: distinct-ok: per-direction author agents (different prompt/allowed-tools); lead_author.invoke_agent is a different agent (lead curation)
entries.load_config: distinct-ok: same split as _config_path — elastic_adapter keeps its own loader because its config keys are not the shared template's (#619)
entries.materialize: distinct-ok: the two eval harnesses build tmp trees from different fixtures (findings/lessons vs query-catalog/run-dir)
entries.read_batch: distinct-ok: lessons reads under the separate .findings.lock (live runs append concurrently); curator reads under the batch-held queue lock — different concurrency model (#386)
entries.rotate_queue: distinct-ok: lessons rotates merge_concurrent=True under .findings.lock; curator rotates merge_concurrent=False (queue lock held across the batch) (#386)
entries.run_batch: distinct-ok: per-direction public entrypoint — lessons + curator each wrap the shared run_batch_envelope with their own inner driver, and the actor/benign modules forward to curator.run_batch (#386)
